strengths: Clearly presents three distinct objects with at least minimal real-world contexts for each
Attempts to link each object to the IA prompt by distinguishing knowledge, belief and opinion
Includes some reference to authority (e.g., mathematiciansâ consensus, historical evidence, manufacturer statistics) to support justifications
weaknesses: Real-world contexts are often implicit or vague, and the videogame screenshot raises questions about the specificity of the MG7 object
Explanations of links to the prompt are superficial, repetitive and lack nuanced or sustained analysis
Evidence is asserted without proper citation or depth, and key TOK concepts are mentioned descriptively rather than analysed precisely
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates the core requirements of the 3â4 band by identifying three objects, offering basic links to the chosen IA prompt and providing some justification for each inclusion. However, the real-world contexts remain imprecise, the connections are repetitive and under-developed, and evidence is largely unsupported. These factors place the work at the upper end of the 3â4 band, justifying a final score of 4.

annotations.comment: Media attention is mentioned without sources; cite reviews, articles or forums that discuss the MG7âs design and performance to ground the discussion.
The exhibition lacks a concluding section synthesizing the three objects; include a summary that reflects on overarching TOK insights and answers the prompt holistically.
The use of first-person narration is personal but could be balanced with more formal academic tone when articulating TOK arguments to enhance objectivity.
Stating âI use this car everydayâ in the game conflates digital usage with real usage; clarify how in-game experience informs knowledge about the actual vehicle.
The exhibition tends to repeat the same analytical framework for each object; vary the structure or depth of analysis to highlight unique insights in each case.
Ensure each section explicitly restates the prompt question or IA prompt to maintain clear alignment and remind the reader of the exhibitionâs focus.
The student critically questions manufacturer statistics, demonstrating a valuable TOK insight into the reliability of authoritative data in a technological context.
The Vietnamese Buddhist bracelet is introduced without precise provenance; specify the Buddhist tradition, maker or region to anchor it as a concrete real-world object.

The studentâs choice of prompt in the title is clear and focused, establishing a coherent thematic framework for the exhibition from the outset. This provides a solid foundation for linking each object to the central TOK question.
Throughout the exhibition, historical and statistical claims lack citations; integrate specific references or data sources to strengthen the evidence base.
The textbook image appears generic; selecting a photograph of the studentâs actual copy (with identifiers) would strengthen the authenticity of the real-world context.
The real-world context of âmy Math AA SL textbookâ is implied but not specifiedâidentify the edition, publication year or classroom setting explicitly to ground this as a distinct object.
The claim that imaginary numbers are ânot necessarily trueâ lacks justification; clarify whether this refers to ontological status, utility or epistemic acceptance.
The analysis of knowledge, belief and opinion in the religious context is repetitive; deepen the discussion by examining ritual authority and communal validation of beliefs.
The description of classroom/home use is descriptive but lacks depth. Consider explaining how specific exercises or collaborations illustrate the nature of mathematical knowledge.
The definition of knowledge as âverified by authorityâ is oversimplified; discuss the role of proof, consensus and axiomatic systems in mathematics for a nuanced analysis.
The section on personal belief in math is incompleteâensure each conceptual category (knowledge, belief, opinion) is fully articulated to avoid abrupt endings.
The binary distinction between proven mathematical knowledge and unproven beliefs/opinions is too rigidâconsider edge cases or historical shifts in what counts as knowledge.
The placebo effect example is insightful but unsubstantiated; include studies or testimonies to support claims about psychological reinforcement in beliefs.
The personal bracelet image is compelling and specific, enhancing the exhibitionâs sense of ownership and distinct real-world context.
The statement that the bracelet existed 3000 years ago lacks citation; incorporate archaeological or textual evidence to substantiate the historical claim.
Introducing âcommunity of knowersâ is a good move, but the concept needs elaborationâexplain how collective endorsement influences belief versus knowledge.
The gaming context is engaging but needs explanation of how digital simulations relate to physical product knowledge and consumer perception.
Using a videogame screenshot as a âreal-world objectâ raises questionsâjustify why the virtual representation of the MG7 qualifies as a specific real item.
While beliefs and opinions are defined, the discussion remains descriptive; connect these categories to TOK concepts like justification and certainty for precision.
The concluding argument about knowledgeâs similarity to belief/opinion is ambitious but convoluted; structure it more clearly, using concrete examples and logical steps.
Key TOK terms such as justification, certainty and authority are mentioned but not unpacked; deepen the conceptual analysis by engaging with these notions explicitly.
Images are large and visually appealing; however, integrate textual analysis of each image to demonstrate how it supports your TOK commentary.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
